evals.name: html-styles
evals.prompt:
Build me a landing page with a dark background (#1a1a2e), white text, a hero section with a centered heading in Inter font, and a CTA button in coral (#ff6b6b). Mobile-first.

[After Claude builds it, user says:]
/verify
evals.expected_output: A structured compliance report that checks each style requirement (background color, text color, hero section, centered heading, Inter font, coral button, mobile-first) against the generated HTML/CSS, with PASS/FAIL verdicts and line-number evidence.

evals.name: partial-compliance
evals.prompt:
Create a card component: rounded corners (12px), subtle box shadow, padding 24px, max-width 400px, and a hover effect that scales up slightly.

[After Claude builds it, user says:]
/verify
evals.expected_output: A compliance report that identifies each requirement and catches any missing ones (especially hover effect if omitted), with specific CSS property evidence for each verdict.

evals.name: scope-creep
evals.prompt:
Write a simple Node.js function that reads a JSON file and returns the parsed object.

[After Claude builds it, user says:]
/verify
evals.expected_output: A compliance report that passes the core requirement (reads JSON, returns parsed object) but flags any unrequested additions (error handling, validation, logging, type checking) as EXTRA with appropriate severity.
